pick.rationale: Tennessee holds a commanding 27-spot advantage in both KenPom and Torvik rankings with a +26.06 adjusted efficiency margin that exceeds SMU's +18.11 mark by nearly 8 points. The Volunteers' elite defense ranked 13th nationally presents a severe challenge for SMU's offense, while Tennessee's offense faces a favorable matchup against SMU's mediocre 95th-ranked defense. With Nate Ament returning strongly from his ankle injury to score 27 points in Tennessee's SEC Tournament victory, the Volunteers enter with superior health and momentum compared to an SMU squad that stumbled down the stretch.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs SMU matchup analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed team ratings, injury status, and recent form for this 6/11 matchup. Tennessee holds significant advantages in defensive efficiency (13th vs 95th) and overall efficiency margin (+26.06 vs +18.11). Both teams returned key players from injury, but Tennessee's elite defense and SEC-tested resume provide decisive edges in a seed line historically prone to upsets.

result.text: Tennessee 72-62 Auburn (Mar 12, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Tennessee Volunteers vs Auburn Tigers SEC Tournament - 2nd Round Auburn Tigers Auburn AUB 17-16 , 7-11 SEC 62 17-16 , 7-11 SEC Final 1 2 T Auburn AUB 32 30 62 Tennessee TENN 25 47 72 25 Tennessee Volunteers Tennessee TENN 22-10 , 11-7 SEC 72 22-10 , 11-7 SEC Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Nate Ament scores 27 as No. 25 Tennessee rallies to beat Auburn at SEC Tournament By AP Updated: Mar 12, 2026, 05:59 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email NASHVILLE, Tenn. -- — Nate Ament scored 27 points as the 25th-ranked Tennessee Volunteers rallied from an 11-point deficit and beat Auburn 72-62 Thursday to reach the Southeastern Conference Tournament quarterfinals. The fifth-seeded Volunteers (22-10) will play No. 22 Vanderbilt on Friday afternoon for a slot in the semifinals. Ament started and played 32 minutes after sitting out the last two games with an injured right knee and ankle . Tennessee wouldn't have had a chance without the freshman. Auburn led 49-39 on a bucket by Keyshawn Hall when Ament started a 22-2 run for Tennessee by scoring the first 10 points. Ja'Kobi Gillespie 's layup then tied it up at 51 with 7:34 left, and Amari Evans ' free throw gave the Vols their first lead since the opening minutes. Ament, who scored 17 of his 27 in the second half, capped the spurt with a free throw off a technical foul on Auburn coach Steven Pearl for a 61-51 lead with 3:56 left. Gillespie finished with 15 points for Tennessee. No. 12 seed Auburn now has lost four of six games, leaving the Tigers (17-16) hoping their strength of schedule and a win at SEC regular-season champ Florida in mid-January pushes them into the NCAA Tournament. Tahaad Pettiford led Auburn with 28 points, and Hall added 10. Pearl said Tennessee “punked” his Tigers on Jan. 31 when the Vols never trailed winning that game. This time, the Tigers controlled the boards early and led 32-25 at halftime. But when Tennessee went on its run, the Tigers turned it over four times, had a shot clock violation and went more than six minutes without a point until Pettiford's 3-pointer with 3:50 left. Up next Auburn goes home to hope and learn its postseason future. Tennessee plays No. 22 Vanderbilt for the second time in seven days. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). result.text: Tennessee basketball injury update, Nate Ament on report vs Vanderbilt Skip to main content Home Sports Titans Predators Nashville SC Vanderbilt UT High Schools Other Colleges Sports Columnist Joe Rexrode NFL Draft Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Tennessee basketball injury update, includes Nate Ament vs Vanderbilt Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Several players appeared on the availability report for Tennessee basketball on March 6. Nate Ament, Bishop Boswell and Troy Henderson all showed up on the report ahead of the No. 25 Vols (21-9, 11-6) regular season finale against No. 22 Vanderbilt (23-7, 10-7) at Food City Center on March 7 (2 p.m. ET, ESPN). Ament , the 6-foot-10 freshman forward, is doubtful with a right leg injury, which Tennessee basketball coach Rick Barnes has described as a high ankle sprain. He missed his first game of the season when the Vols defeated South Carolina, 78-59, on March 3. Starting guard Bishop Boswell and reserve guard Troy Henderson are both probable. Henderson has been dealing with a shoulder injury that initially kept him out of Tennessee's win at Alabama on Jan. 24. Boswell has an illness that prevented from being at practice on March 6. Reserve freshman guard Clarence Massamba is questionable to play with a right hip injury that has sidelined him unavailable for several games. Wynton Jackson covers high school sports for Knox News. You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: Tennessee 72-62 Auburn (Mar 12, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Tennessee Volunteers vs Auburn Tigers SEC Tournament - 2nd Round Auburn Tigers Auburn AUB 17-16 , 7-11 SEC 62 17-16 , 7-11 SEC Final 1 2 T Auburn AUB 32 30 62 Tennessee TENN 25 47 72 25 Tennessee Volunteers Tennessee TENN 22-10 , 11-7 SEC 72 22-10 , 11-7 SEC Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Nate Ament scores 27 as No. 25 Tennessee rallies to beat Auburn at SEC Tournament By AP Updated: Mar 12, 2026, 05:59 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email NASHVILLE, Tenn. -- — Nate Ament scored 27 points as the 25th-ranked Tennessee Volunteers rallied from an 11-point deficit and beat Auburn 72-62 Thursday to reach the Southeastern Conference Tournament quarterfinals. The fifth-seeded Volunteers (22-10) will play No. 22 Vanderbilt on Friday afternoon for a slot in the semifinals. Ament started and played 32 minutes after sitting out the last two games with an injured right knee and ankle . Tennessee wouldn't have had a chance without the freshman. Auburn led 49-39 on a bucket by Keyshawn Hall when Ament started a 22-2 run for Tennessee by scoring the first 10 points. Ja'Kobi Gillespie 's layup then tied it up at 51 with 7:34 left, and Amari Evans ' free throw gave the Vols their first lead since the opening minutes. Ament, who scored 17 of his 27 in the second half, capped the spurt with a free throw off a technical foul on Auburn coach Steven Pearl for a 61-51 lead with 3:56 left. Gillespie finished with 15 points for Tennessee. No. 12 seed Auburn now has lost four of six games, leaving the Tigers (17-16) hoping their strength of schedule and a win at SEC regular-season champ Florida in mid-January pushes them into the NCAA Tournament. Tahaad Pettiford led Auburn with 28 points, and Hall added 10. Pearl said Tennessee “punked” his Tigers on Jan. 31 when the Vols never trailed winning that game. This time, the Tigers controlled the boards early and led 32-25 at halftime. But when Tennessee went on its run, the Tigers turned it over four times, had a shot clock violation and went more than six minutes without a point until Pettiford's 3-pointer with 3:50 left. Up next Auburn goes home to hope and learn its postseason future. Tennessee plays No. 22 Vanderbilt for the second time in seven days. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). AP college basketball: https://apnews.com/hub/ap-top-25-college-basketball-poll and https://apnews.com/hub/college-basketball Game Information Nashville , TN SEC Network Referee : Chuck Jones Referee : Joe Lindsay Referee : Vladimir Voyard-Tadal 2025-26 Standings NCAA Division I Southeastern Conference Southeastern Conference CONF GB OVR Florida 16-2 - 26-7 Arkansas 13-5 3 26-8 Alabama

result.text: Tennessee basketball injury update, Nate Ament on report vs Vanderbilt Skip to main content Home Sports Titans Predators Nashville SC Vanderbilt UT High Schools Other Colleges Sports Columnist Joe Rexrode NFL Draft Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Tennessee basketball injury update, includes Nate Ament vs Vanderbilt Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Several players appeared on the availability report for Tennessee basketball on March 6. Nate Ament, Bishop Boswell and Troy Henderson all showed up on the report ahead of the No. 25 Vols (21-9, 11-6) regular season finale against No. 22 Vanderbilt (23-7, 10-7) at Food City Center on March 7 (2 p.m. ET, ESPN). Ament , the 6-foot-10 freshman forward, is doubtful with a right leg injury, which Tennessee basketball coach Rick Barnes has described as a high ankle sprain. He missed his first game of the season when the Vols defeated South Carolina, 78-59, on March 3. Starting guard Bishop Boswell and reserve guard Troy Henderson are both probable. Henderson has been dealing with a shoulder injury that initially kept him out of Tennessee's win at Alabama on Jan. 24. Boswell has an illness that prevented from being at practice on March 6. Reserve freshman guard Clarence Massamba is questionable to play with a right hip injury that has sidelined him unavailable for several games. Wynton Jackson covers high school sports for Knox News. **Tennessee:**
- Ranked 15th in both KenPom and Torvik
- Record: 22-11 (22-10 at time of Auburn game)

- Elite defense (13th nationally in adjusted defensive efficiency)
- Good offense (33rd-35th nationally)
- Adjusted efficiency margin: +26.06
- Key player Nate Ament returned from injury (high ankle sprain) and scored 27 points vs Auburn in SEC Tournament
- SEC Tournament: Beat Auburn 72-62, then likely lost to Vanderbilt (based on bracket progression)

**SMU:**
- Ranked 42nd in both KenPom and Torvik
- Record: 20-13 (9-11 in ACC)
- Good offense (26th-27th nationally in adjusted offensive efficiency)
- Mediocre defense (95th nationally)
- Adjusted efficiency margin: +18.11
- BJ Edwards (their star player: 12.7 PPG, 5.9 RPG, 4.9 APG) was injured with ankle injury but reportedly returning for tournament
- Lost 4 straight games to end regular season
- Beat Miami (OH) in First Four (per prior picks)

**Analysis:**
This is a 6/11 matchup. Tennessee has a 27-spot ranking advantage (15 vs 42). The key differentiator is defense - Tennessee has an elite defense (13th) while SMU's defense is mediocre (95th). Both teams have good offenses (Tennessee 33rd, SMU 26th).

Tennessee enters healthier - Nate Ament returned and played well. SMU was struggling with BJ Edwards' injury, though he was expected to return for the tournament.

The efficiency margin gap is significant: Tennessee +26.06 vs SMU +18.11 - nearly an 8-point difference.


This matchup clearly favors Tennessee. The Volunteers' defensive prowess and consistent performance throughout the season give them a substantial edge. Despite SMU's offensive capabilities, their defensive vulnerabilities and recent struggles with injuries make them a significant underdog.
